user_prompt: Make a `lib\WifiManager\AsyncFsManager.cpp` and its associated header file
- init, called from any activation of Wi-Fi
- reset walk, reset iterator for file listing, should always work no matter what state
- walk one, return one file from directory, the next call should return the next file, return something to indicate end of list when needed, automatically restart the walk if something is wrong
- open file for download, closes any other file or anything else that might conflict, reset seek pointer
critically, all of these need to be thread safe
web server code can only use methods within this manager, add thread safe functions to this manager as needed
use it to attempt to fix the problem with listing files and the decrypted download
agent_reply: Implemented `AsyncFsManager` and routed the web SD access through it.
What changed:
- Added [AsyncFsManager.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/AsyncFsManager.h:19) and [AsyncFsManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/AsyncFsManager.cpp:93).
- It owns the active walk handle and active download handle behind a recursive FreeRTOS mutex.
- `resetWalk`, `walkOne`, `closeWalk`, `openFileForDownload`, `readFileChunk`, and `closeFile` are all serialized.
- Opening a download closes any active walk; resetting a walk closes any active download.
- Added thread-safe helpers for web upload/delete/readiness: `writeFileChunk`, `removeFile`, `isFile`, `isReady`.
I also wired:
- `/list_files.json` to use `AsyncFsManager::resetWalk()` and `walkOne()` instead of holding its own `FsFile`: [WebFileHandlers.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp:221)
- `/download_file` to use `openFileForDownload()` / `readFileChunk()`: [WebFileHandlers.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp:413)
- decrypted download to use the manager for encrypted recording reads: [DecryptedDownload.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/DecryptedDownload.cpp:505)
- Wi-Fi activation reset via `AsyncFsManager::init()`: [WifiManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp:397)
Verification: `pio run -e thefly_sec1` passes.

user_prompt: the shared resource is SPI, I bet it's being interrupted by a GUI redraw
so thread safety isn't just about file system objects, it needs to work well with `FlyGuiView`'s polling calls
agent_reply: Agreed, and I implemented the first blunt-but-useful version of that coordination.
What changed:
- `AsyncFsManager::guiShouldYield()` now reports when a file walk or download is active.
- `FlyGui::poll()` now returns immediately while the async FS manager is active, so GUI redraw/touch polling won’t compete with SD reads during web listing/download.
- The reopen-and-retry SD read path I added before the interruption is still in place, with SD error code/data logging.
Build passes: `pio run -e thefly_sec1`.

This is intentionally conservative: during a browser file download, the on-device GUI may feel frozen until the transfer ends or disconnects. If this fixes the download corruption, we can refine it into a real shared SPI arbiter or timed “download quiet mode” instead of a broad GUI yield.
